message:
Add Mutter Butter design spec

Initial design for a GNOME Shell extension that implements grid-based
window management with live-scaling postage stamp behavior for inactive
windows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,142 @@
+# Mutter Butter — Design Spec
+
+A GNOME Shell extension that anchors windows to a configurable grid with live-scaling "postage stamp" behavior for inactive windows.
+
+## Overview
+
+Mutter Butter replaces GNOME's default window placement with a grid-based system where:
+
+- Windows snap to a configurable NxM grid (per monitor)
+- Windows that fall below a configurable focus-rank threshold automatically shrink to postage-stamp size
+- Shrunk windows are live-rendered miniatures (Clutter actor scaling, no content reflow)
+- Users can manually zoom any window through discrete scale steps
+- Windows can be marked exempt from auto-shrinking
+- The result is a persistent, live Exposé where windows coexist at different scale factors
+
+## Architecture
+
+Monolithic GNOME Shell extension targeting GNOME 45+ (ES modules style). Six internal modules:
+
+| Module | Responsibility |
+|---|---|
+| `gridEngine` | Per-monitor grid management, cell allocation, window-to-cell mapping |
+| `scaleManager` | Clutter actor scale transforms, per-window scaleFactor + scaleSource tracking |
+| `focusTracker` | Ordered focus-rank list, triggers auto-shrink/restore |
+| `stampPlacer` | Three stamp placement strategies (in-place, shelf, pack) |
+| `dragHandler` | Window drag override, grid snapping at scaled dimensions |
+| `settings` | GSettings schema, libadwaita preferences UI |
+
+### Data Flow
+
+1. User focuses a window → `focusTracker` updates rank list
+2. Window drops below rank N → `focusTracker` tells `scaleManager` to auto-shrink
+3. `scaleManager` applies Clutter `scale_x`/`scale_y` transform to `MetaWindowActor`
+4. `stampPlacer` repositions the stamp per active placement strategy
+5. `gridEngine` recalculates cell occupancy using scaled dimensions
+
+## Grid System
+
+- **Configuration:** NxM cells per monitor, configurable in preferences UI
+- **Per-monitor:** Each monitor has its own independent grid
+- **Cross-monitor drag:** Windows can be dragged between monitor grids
+- **Cell occupancy:** Calculated using scaled dimensions, rounded up. A window that occupies 4x3 cells at 100% occupies 2x2 cells at 50% (ceil(4*0.5) x ceil(3*0.5)). Minimum occupancy is 1x1 cell.
+- **New window placement:** Grid engine finds best-fit free cells; if none available, lowest-ranked window auto-shrinks to make room
+
+## Scale System
+
+### Scale Steps
+
+Four discrete scale levels: 100% → 75% → 50% → 25%
+
+Cycled via `Super+Plus` (zoom in) and `Super+Minus` (zoom out).
+
+### Scale Source: Auto vs Manual
+
+Every window tracks three properties:
+
+- `scaleFactor`: Current scale (1.0, 0.75, 0.5, 0.25)
+- `scaleSource`: `auto` or `manual`
+- `previousScaleFactor`: Scale before auto-shrink (used for restore). Only meaningful when scaleSource=auto.
+
+**Auto-shrunk windows** (scaleSource=auto): Caused by dropping below focus rank N. `previousScaleFactor` is saved before shrinking. Restored to `previousScaleFactor` automatically when the window regains focus. Focusing an auto-shrunk stamp brings it back and bumps the lowest-ranked window down to stamp. A window that was manually at 75% and then auto-shrunk to 25% restores to 75%.
+
+**Manually-scaled windows** (scaleSource=manual): Set when user explicitly zooms with keybinds. Stays at user-set scale even when focused. User chose this size intentionally.
+
+### Clutter Transform Details
+
+- Set `scale_x` and `scale_y` on the window's `MetaWindowActor`/Clutter actor
+- Pivot point at top-left corner (0, 0) so the window shrinks toward its grid position
+- Clutter automatically handles input coordinate transformation for scaled actors
+- Content renders at full resolution, just visually scaled — no reflow, no resize events sent to the client
+
+### Exempt Windows
+
+- Toggled via right-click on title bar → "Exempt from auto-shrink" menu item
+- Exempt windows never auto-shrink regardless of focus rank
+- Exempt windows can still be manually zoomed with keybinds
+- Exemption state persists via GSettings (keyed by WM_CLASS or app ID)
+
+## Stamp Placement Modes
+
+Three strategies, selectable in preferences UI:
+
+### In-Place
+
+Window stays at its current grid position, just occupies fewer cells. Surrounding grid space is freed. No windows move.
+
+### Shelf
+
+Stamps migrate to a configurable edge of the monitor (bottom, top, left, or right). They line up along the shelf edge. Frees their original grid cells entirely. If the shelf is full (stamps exceed available edge cells), additional stamps wrap to the next row/column inward from the edge.
+
+### Pack
+
+Stamps bin-pack into available free cells in the grid, filling gaps left by other windows. Optimizes for minimal wasted grid space.
+
+## Key Behaviors
+
+| Event | Behavior |
+|---|---|
+| Focus an auto-shrunk stamp | Restore to previous scale; bump lowest-ranked to stamp |
+| Focus a manually-shrunk stamp | Gets focus, stays at current scale |
+| Super+Plus / Super+Minus | Cycle scale steps on focused window; sets scaleSource=manual |
+| Right-click title bar → Exempt | Toggle exempt flag; exempt windows never auto-shrink |
+| Drag a scaled window | Snaps to grid at scaled size; preview overlay shows target cells |
+| New window opens | Grid engine finds best-fit free cells; if none, lowest-ranked auto-shrinks |
+| Window closes | Freed cells become available; no automatic unshrinking |
+| Monitor added/removed | Grid recalculates; displaced windows re-placed on remaining monitors |
+
+## Settings (GSettings Schema)
+
+| Key | Type | Default | Description |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `grid-columns` | int | 8 | Number of grid columns per monitor |
+| `grid-rows` | int | 6 | Number of grid rows per monitor |
+| `focus-rank-threshold` | int | 3 | Number of top-ranked windows that stay full size |
+| `scale-steps` | array of double | [1.0, 0.75, 0.5, 0.25] | Available scale steps |
+| `placement-mode` | enum | 'in-place' | Stamp placement strategy: 'in-place', 'shelf', 'pack' |
+| `shelf-edge` | enum | 'bottom' | Edge for shelf placement mode |
+| `exempt-apps` | array of string | [] | WM_CLASS / app IDs exempt from auto-shrink |
+| `zoom-in-keybind` | string | '\<Super\>plus' | Keybind to zoom in |
+| `zoom-out-keybind` | string | '\<Super\>minus' | Keybind to zoom out |
+
+## Preferences UI
+
+Standard libadwaita `Adw.PreferencesWindow` with groups for:
+
+- **Grid:** Column/row spinners per monitor
+- **Behavior:** Focus rank threshold spinner, placement mode dropdown, shelf edge dropdown
+- **Keybindings:** Zoom in/out keybind editors
+- **Exempt Apps:** List of exempt applications with add/remove
+
+## Technology
+
+- **Language:** GJS (GNOME JavaScript), ES modules
+- **APIs:** Meta, Clutter, St, Gio (GSettings)
+- **Settings UI:** Adw.PreferencesWindow (libadwaita)
+- **Target GNOME versions:** 45, 46, 47
+- **Testing:** Unit tests for grid engine and stamp placement (pure JS, runnable outside Shell). Integration testing via nested Mutter session (`mutter --nested`).
+
+## Limitations
+
+- External tools querying window geometry via Wayland/X11 protocol will see the original (unscaled) window size. The extension's grid, input handling, and all GNOME Shell interactions respect the scaled size, but `wmctrl`, `xdotool`, etc. cannot be spoofed from the JS layer.
+- Windows at 25% scale are small — content identification relies on the live miniature render alone (no icon/title overlay per user preference).
